Notes: Opening night insanity (i.e. very enthusiastic audience involving a lot of yelling and cheering). The show stopped after technical difficulties through Here For You, and again, Dolly Parton came out to talk to the audience AND sing 9 to 5. There are a few little bloopers and ad-libs. My favorite is when Marc accidentally knocked over the pencils when falling back in his chair, and Megan picked them up. Then when he was supposed to deliberately knock over the pencils, Megan went to pick them up again and said, Just keeps happening, don't it? SJB still sounds a bit sick, but she used it to her advantage and did a crazy fierce Get Out and Stay Out. I was just about on the floor in amazement. A lot of little changes, too. The big one for the master was that they got rid of the dialogue between Andy Karl and Allison Janney in the lunch room scene—now all he does is trip, and then he slinks off.

Notes: Final performance! Filmed in 16:9 widescreen. Few obstructions, only in the wide-shots when there are two heads on either side of the frame, but filmer does his/her best to shoot around them. External audio fed into video from MD recorder, so audio is very clear. A mix of close-ups and wider shots when multiple people were on stage to see their interactions. This was a gut-wrenchingly sad performance. Most of the cast members were in tears at some point or another during the show. In particular, Stephanie was sobbing through I Just Might and Allison started sobbing at the end of One of the Boys. A few minor ad-libs and mishaps. One being when Allison pulled out a tape measure during the urinals bit of One of the Boys. The cast was sad but they were clearly trying to have the times of their lives and go out with a bang. And they fed off the audience energy, which was insane and very supportive.

Notes:
The stars of the NBC musical drama series Smash reunite virtually for a special one-night-only streaming event: Bombshell in Concert. The event, announced by The Actors Fund in association with PEOPLE, features the never-seen sold-out live performance of Bombshell in Concert that electrified Broadway at the Minskoff Theater in New York City in 2015.
The evening was introduced by two-time Academy Award winner Renée Zellweger, and during intermission, Julie Klausner of “Difficult People” hosted a live, virtual reunion with the show's original cast members, including: Christian Borle, Jaime Cepero, Will Chase, Brian d'Arcy James, Jack Davenport, Ann Harada, Megan Hilty, Jeremy Jordan, Katharine McPhee, Andy Mientus, Debra Messing, Leslie Odom Jr., Krysta Rodriguez and Wesley Taylor.
The People version of the proshot cut the song At Your Feet.

Notes: Incredible performances all around, but the stand out is an absolutely electric performance by Cynthia. She gives a Tony-worthy performance, and there is a standing ovation for her after I'm Here as well as at the end of the show. A nice capture overall. There is a one minute blackout during Huckleberry Pie and a two minute blackout during Our Prayer; the rest of the show is fully captured with just a couple quick dropouts. There is one head that occasionally blocks the front left section of the stage, but it's worked around whenever possible. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans. 2 discs.

Performance Notes: A just-about-perfect capture of a hugely significant evening for Marianne Elliott’s Company: the first performance following the Tony Award nomination ceremony where the production became the most nominated revival of the season. Matt Doyle's debut Tony nomination, EIGHT-time Tony-nominee Patti LuPone, Katina Lenk taking on her deafening snub with one of the best performances of this character I've ever seen, AND MOST IMPORTANTLY, it is the very performance that gave us Chris Harper pays my salary. (The video features a modified pre-show and post-show speech from Rashidra Scott, acknowledging the talkback following the performance.) When people say bootlegs are the preservation of theatrical history, this is what they're fuckin talking about.

Technical Notes: Stageshot. Excellent capture. No obstructions. Almost no washout. No dropouts. Excellent audio (though is apologetically bombarded by frequent scream laughing from me.) Theres a brief show stop immediately after the title number, and the video captures the curtain coming down and the resuming of the performance. The first act unfortunately loses its picture a few minutes after Not Getting Married today - especially unfortunate as the performance was one of the last which featured rain during Marry Me a Little. Shot from front row stage left. [A]
Performance Notes: This production is the most premium musical theatre experience I have ever had in my life. I could simply go on for hours about how magnificent this entire production was - Katrinas bottomlessly fleshed-out interpretation of Bobbie, Patti LuPones masterclass display of performing, Jennifer Simards nomination-worthy comedy, Christopher Siebers perfect matching and prodding of his scene partner, Claybourne Elders show-stealing acting, Bobby Contes booming vocals, Marianne Elliots ability to make this total re-envisioning utterly airtight dramaturgically, Bunny Christies masterful and detailed set design, Neil Austins exacting lighting design executed to meticulous precision, Ian Dickinsons complex sound design. This is a thorough gift to Broadway and this video captures a performance at its absolutely perfect settling point.

Master Notes: Great 4k capture of this touring cast!  Filmed with a mix of mostly wides and mediums with a few close-ups.  After a slightly lengthy interval, it was announced that Randy Cain would be taking over as Hades and Darius Manuel would be swinging on as a worker.  Some slight graininess to the picture at times and the very far left of the stage is somewhat obstructed by the lighting rig, including the passage that acts as the entrance/exit to Hadestown.  Occasional wandering/readjusting throughout, but little obstruction except when the person in front of me fidgeted with their hair.  Slight amount of camera fog at the beginning, and there’s an extended dropout during Anyway the Wind Blows due to latecomers in my row.  General warning for intense strobing lights, timestamp: 1:22:58-1:23:10.  Sorry for crying like all throughout Road to Hell (Reprise) but Jaylon Hermes…they really got to me.  Includes We Raise Our Cups.

Notes:
Amber Gray's final performance in Hadestown. Full show is filmed, including the standing ovation that Amber got before the performance even started, right up to the band exiting. Moderate obstruction from the box/bar, etc. Dropouts and shakiness during applause breaks, especially during all of the standing ovations that happened for Amber—what can I say? For transparency, some screenshots include the obstructed/less-than-HQ moments, so people know what they're purchasing/watching.

Notes:
Pro-shot filmed in May 2022 with the Off-West End cast, and released on September 16, 2022. Originally released on Roku. Veronica Sawyer does her best to survive her senior year while navigating the beautiful but cruel Heathers, the new to school Jason J.D. Dean, and the constant pressure to fit in with everyone else.
Three versions exist, the one released on Roku, the one released in UK cinemas without ad breaks or BTS moments, and one that contains a BTS of the filming process with no ad breaks.
Multiple Subtitle files exist to correspond to the timing of the different versions.

Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Megan Hilty (s/b Glinda), Joey McIntyre (Fiyero), George Hearn (The Wizard), Carole Shelley (Madame Morrible), Michelle Federer (Nessarose), Christopher Fitzgerald (Boq), William Youmans (Doctor Dillamond)
Notes:
The most common fileset (MP3 tracked - 195 MB) that usually circulates as this recording is incorrect, as it actually features Jennifer Laura Thompson, and seems to be dated November 28, 2004. The correct audio with Megan Hilty is MP3 tracked - 291 MB and ends after For Good. Slightly quiet, not as bad on the overload as many early Wicked audios.
This audio does frequently skip a few seconds, more so in Act 2 than Act 1. In Act 1, this happens at the following points: twice in the Room Assignments scene (first from Glinda's “You can all come visit me!” to Elphaba's “Madame Morrible, we have not yet received our room assignments”, then from Elphaba's “But I promised Father!” to “Something just comes over me sometimes”), in The Wizard and I (from “help me meet the Wizard” to “meet the Wizard, once I prove my worth”), in What Is This Feeling (from “my roommate is” to “Does it have a name?”), in the scene before Something Bad (from Elphaba's “Animals should be seen and not heard?!” to Dillamond's “if only it were just a matter of words on a chalkboard”), in the transition after Something Bad (skips to Fiyero's “I'm sure I won't last longer at this school than any of the others”), in Defying Gravity (from “But 'til I try, I'll never know” to the guard's “In the name of His Supreme Ozness!”).
Act 2 starts a bit into No One Mourns the Wicked (Reprise) (from “where'er she goes”), and skips at the following points: in Thank Goodness (from “share our ending vicariously” to the first “what happens when all your dreams come true”), in The Wicked Witch of the East (from “just the girl in the mirror” to Nessarose's “Elphaba, please don't leave me!”), in Wonderful (from “dime-a-dozen mediocrities” to “got carried away”), twice in the Catfight scene (first from Glinda's “to have a house fall on you” to “maybe not an accident”, then from Glinda's “taking things that don't belong to us” to “Yes, I do” after the slap), seven times in No Good Deed (first from the start of Elphaba's chanting to “flesh not be torn”, then from “Let him never die” to “I don't even know what I'm reading”, then from “Already dead or bleeding?” to the first “No good deed goes unpunished”, then from “That's my new creed” to “Nessa...”, then from the second “Fiyero!” to “seeking good”, then from the fourth “No good deed goes unpunished” to “what well-meant did”, then from the final “No good-” to the middle of the last note), seven times in March of the Witch Hunters (jumping straight to the first “kill her”, then skipping the word “and” in the second “find her and kill her”, and then going from “Evil effectively-” to Boq's “With the Witch!”, from Boq's “I'm not the only one” to “grievance to repay”, then skipping Morrible's “Yes, I suppose it was just her time” and “And now you're getting what you want!”, and finally going from “I would join you if I could” to “But good!”), five times in the Kiamo Ko scene (from Dorothy's crying to Elphaba's “see your Aunt Em”, then from Elphaba mocking Dorothy's crying to “Chistery, there you are”, skipping Glinda's “I know you don’t want to hear this but somebody’s got to say it: You are out of control! I mean, come on, they're just shoes, let it go!”, then going from Glinda's “It's Fiyero, isn't it?” to Elphaba's “I surrender”, then from Glinda's “I don't care!” to “I don't understand”), and in For Good (from Elphaba's “Just look at me” to “You can do all I couldn't do”).

Multiple copies circulate:
VOB + Smalls (3.65 GB) - 1st generation from the master. 1 Disc. Unfortunately, the actual tape transfer is rather bad and results in a lot of washout. Includes extras: NY1 Review Segment, Today Show performance and Letterman performance.
VOB + Smalls (4.80 GB) & (7.46 GB) - 2 Disc copy. Noted as the better version of this video. Unfortunately, the color is semi off and the picture is rather soft.

Master Notes:
Last three caps are from the original DV capture shot in PAL. It's a 21 year old video. If it were a child, I'd be buying it a drink now, so... the quality was great for its time, and it's an improvement over what's floating around.

This was the first full length video I'd filmed, so there was plenty of upper body cramping, which is the reason for the camera shaking, but I thin k it was the only video that came out with Idina/Taye, so we take what we can :-)
Notes:
Restored to the original PAL, though not without a hit from the gen loss incurred along the way. Audio has been filtered to remove the camera noise and excess hiss from the theatre. Image is on the shaky side, and the camera has a hard time locking into focus, combined with being out of sync with the US lights leading to a constant flicker. A pity because its a strong performance by the original cast during the show's ascent.
Sometimes mislabeled as September 1, 2004; Taye was only in the show from December 22 2003 - January 18 2004 while Norbert Leo Butz was out due to a back injury. The audio circulating as January 8, 2004 is ripped from this video, but the audio rip is better quality than the audio in the video.
As of 2025, the master has confirmed that they have the original unconverted recording, which is better quality than the circulating version(s).

Notes:

Multiple copies are circulating. The best is the 7.8 GB file, which has higher video resolution, working menus, and the playbill. The 7.63 GB file has the same video quality but no playbill, skips 1 minute in late Act 1 (A Sentimental Man (Reprise)), skips the first 1.5 minutes of Act 2, and the smalls may glitch such that the last section of Act 1 doesn't play at all. The 4.07 GB file has worse video quality but does have a working menu, and includes the playbill and various extras (TV spots, Macy's special, Seth's Broadway Chatterbox).

Cast: Megan Hilty (Performer), Julia Murney (Performer)
Notes: This was supposed to be a Kristin Chenoweth concert but due to an illness, these two stepped in, which made for some great jokes about Kristin. The two have great chemistry and it's their first time performing together. It was really a sensational concert with great stories and wonderful vocals that must be heard. Songs include: All That Jazz, Back to Before, Ring Them Bells, Till There Was You, I Could Have Danced All Night, Part of Your World, The Wizard and I, Popular, Defying Gravity, For Good, and I Will Never Leave You. A+
